item.title: Apparent hantavirus outbreak kills 3, sickens at least 3 more on cruise ship
item.description: A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus on a cruise ship in the Atlantic off Africa killed three people and sickened at least three others, health officials say.

item.title: Shooting at lake near Oklahoma City leaves at least 12 injured
item.description: At least 12 people were at hospitals following a shooting at party at Arcadia Lake, near Oklahoma City, police say.

item.title: U.S. beginning effort to "guide" stranded ships out of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump announced the U.S. would "guide" ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz. A U.S.-led task force says it has started the operation.

item.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, spokesperson says
item.description: There was no immediate reason given for the 81-year-old's hospitalization.

item.title: Video shows United flight strike truck on N.J. Turnpike before landing at Newark
item.description: An investigation is underway after a United Airlines plane struck a light pole and truck on the New Jersey Turnpike as it was coming in for a landing at Newark Liberty Airport on Sunday afternoon, officials said.

item.title: 5/3/2026: Disaster Tourists; Birds Of War; Perfume Capital Of The World
item.description: First, when the volunteer helping after a hurricane is a white nationalist. Then, birders flock here since guerrillas disarmed. And, Grasse: The perfume capital of the world.

item.title: 5/3: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Passengers deal with Spirit shutdown fallout; Trump's poll numbers tank as Iran war continues to jack up gas prices.

item.title: Golden Tempo wins 152nd Kentucky Derby, making history for its trainer
item.description: Cherie DeVaux became the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

item.title: A day later, Cherie DeVaux reflects on being first woman trainer to win Kentucky Derby
item.description: Trainer Cherie DeVaux says she's still coming to grips with her horse Golden Tempo's longshot win in Saturday's Kentucky Derby. Jericka Duncan reports.

item.title: What does Spirit Airlines' shutdown mean for travelers?
item.description: Have tickets to fly on Spirit? Here's what to know about refunds and alternative flights now that the budget airline is ceasing operations this.

item.title: Replacing America's Minuteman III nuclear missiles
item.description: The United States is in the process of replacing the 60-year-old Minuteman III nuclear missile with the new Sentinel missile. Ian Lee reports, with an exclusive look at one of our nation's most secure and destructive weapons.

item.title: 2 U.S. service members missing amid training exercise in Morocco
item.description: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.

item.title: Fuel prices making wildfire battles more expensive
item.description: Aircraft used to fight wildfires are getting more expensive as fuel prices rise. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: 2 missing U.S. soldiers in Morocco fell off cliff in hiking accident, official says
item.description: A U.S. official speaking on the condition of anonymity said Sunday that two U.S. service members missing in Morocco fell off a cliff into the ocean in what's being described as a hiking accident. They were in the North African country for training exercises. Chris Livesay has more.

item.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after failed effort at government rescue deal
item.description: The budget carrier Spirit Airlines is ceasing operations after failing to land a $500 million bailout from the Trump administration.

item.title: Trump's poll numbers tank as Iran war continues to jack up gas prices
item.description: Prolonged uncertainty about the war in Iran coupled with rising energy and grocery prices are pummeling pocketbooks and President Trump's polling numbers. Olivia Gazis reports.

item.title: Passengers dealing with fallout from Spirit shutdown; fuel prices plaguing other airlines
item.description: Spirit Airlines' abrupt closure on Saturday has left passengers scrambling. And the skyrocketing fuel prices that helped bring about Spirit's end are wreaking havoc on the rest of the industry. Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: United flight clips light pole on Jersey Turnpike while landing at Newark
item.description: A United Boeing 767 arriving from Venice, Italy, struck a light pole on the New Jersey Turnpike while landing at Newark on Sunday, the FAA says. Jericka Duncan hs more.

item.title: Press gala shooting suspect moved off suicide watch, records show
item.description: In a motion requesting Allen's removal from suicide watch, his lawyers said that the restrictions amount to "violations of his rights under the Due Process Clause."

item.title: Man arrested at Trump Miami golf club for disturbance, Secret Service says
item.description: The incident occurred one week after shots were fired during the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington, D.C., and Mr. Trump was rushed off the dais.

item.title: Anderson Cooper learns the thrills of birding
item.description: Anderson Cooper discovers how rare birds, expert guides, and wild landscapes can turn a skeptic into a passionate birder in the mountains of Colombia.

item.title: How natural disasters are exploited to manipulate people online
item.description: John Kelly, head of the social media analytics firm Graphika, showed 60 Minutes correspondent Lesley Stahl how hate groups, foreign governments, and influencers exploit natural disasters to manipulate people on social media.

item.title: Why some white nationalists are showing up to areas hit by natural disasters
item.description: After natural disasters, white nationalists, militias, and conspiracists often arrive, offering help. But they also want to recruit and improve their image.

item.title: When the volunteer helping after a hurricane is a white nationalist | 60 Minutes
item.description: After natural disasters, white nationalists, militias, and conspiracists often arrive, offering help. But they also want to recruit and improve their image.

item.title: Historian Jill Lepore on constitutional amendments | 60 Minutes
item.description: As the U.S. prepares to mark the 250th anniversary of American independence, historian Jill Lepore says now is a good time to ask if the public wants to see constitutional amendments.

item.title: Inside Grasse, where flowers for Chanel No. 5 have been grown for more than a century
item.description: Centuries ago, the French town of Grasse reeked of pungent odors from leather tanning. But now it's famous for quite the opposite scent from the acres of jasmine that it grows for top perfume houses.

item.title: Birding takes flight after peace deal between Colombia and guerrilla group | 60 Minutes
item.description: In Colombia, decades of fighting between the government, left-wing guerrillas, right-wing paramilitaries, and narco-traffickers preserved bird habitats in Colombia.

item.title: Watch a Spirit pilot's impromptu retirement celebration after airline shutters
item.description: A Spirit pilot received an impromptu retirement party from a different airline after what would have been his final flight was canceled.

item.title: Drugmaker asks Supreme Court to halt ruling blocking online access to mifepristone
item.description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone​ asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug just a day earlier.

item.title: What states could try to redistrict and add more GOP seats
item.description: The Supreme Court's ruling on Wednesday about Louisiana's congressional map could have implications for several states, as it narrowed the section of the Voting Rights Act about majority-minority districts.
